expectations.text: Argues the claim is overstated or a measurement artifact
expectations.evidence: States it's 'partly a measurement artifact rather than a complete shift in muscle roles.'

expectations.text: Explains the long head triceps biarticular mechanism
expectations.evidence: States: 'The triceps are a biarticular muscle (crossing both the shoulder and elbow)' — correctly identifies triceps as biarticular.

expectations.text: Cites evidence that benching grows lateral triceps more than long head
expectations.evidence: Does not distinguish between lateral and long head triceps growth from benching.

expectations.text: References data (e.g., Duffy's dissertation) showing pecs/delts/lateral triceps increase activation similarly
expectations.evidence: Does not reference Duffy's dissertation or specific research data.

expectations.text: Practical takeaway: don't abandon pec work; train bench-relevant musculature broadly
expectations.evidence: States: 'Don't abandon pec work' and 'both muscle groups remain critical.'
